rationale: The agent read PR #2 and the diff, identified several genuine bugs (displaced docstring, temp file leak, missing dependency, f-string compat, no timeout, no tests), and attempted pull_request_review_write with state=REQUEST_CHANGES (blocked by read-only PAT, as expected). However, the review body MISSED the two required security issues from expected_behavior: (1) SSRF attack surface — requests.get(url) without URL scheme/host allowlisting (file://, localhost, 169.254.169.254, RFC1918), and (2) no max_bytes cap enabling arbitrary large files to exhaust disk. The expected_behavior explicitly requires the body to '命中 SSRF 或 size cap 至少一个' (hit SSRF or size cap at least once). The temp file leak (issue #2) was found but neither SSRF nor the size/resource exhaustion issue was mentioned. This constitutes a substantive failure to meet the task's explicit security requirements, not just a blocked write.
